scope.summary: F-LOW DB2 verified already satisfied: order_named_binds_for_driver returns Result and rejects missing or leftover names with a typed DbError::NamedBindMismatch before any driver call. This close records verification, not new work.
out_of_scope: Any code change. The positional-append behaviour the finding describes is gone; re-implementing it would be duplicate work.
Bind handling on the plsql-intelligence and pool paths, which do not use this ordering function.

entry_points.name: order_named_binds_for_driver
entry_points.trace: connection.rs:2207-2233 collects `missing` placeholders and leftover supplied names and returns DbError::NamedBindMismatch instead of appending positionally; both call sites (5861, 5896) propagate with `?` before any driver I/O. Tests pass and cover the bead's cases verbatim: named_bind_ordering_rejects_missing_and_unexpected_names asserts BOTH negative cases (':a,:b' supplied ':a,:unused' -> missing=[b], unexpected=[:unused]; ':a' with an extra ':unused' -> missing empty, unexpected=[:unused]), and named_binds_are_ordered_by_first_real_placeholder asserts the positive case with out-of-order, repeated, and case-varied placeholders while ignoring binds inside literals, comments, and quoted identifiers.

limitations: Verification of pre-existing state: I read the cited code and ran the tests; I did not re-derive which commit replaced the positional append.
Static/local only. No live Oracle round trip was made, so 'never calls the driver' is established by the `?` propagation before the driver call, not by observing a driver that was not invoked.
Scoped verification: cargo test -p oraclemcp-db --lib. No workspace-wide run.
